positioningFacts.value: Agility positions Digit as a fully autonomous tool with proven commercial deployments for manufacturing, logistics and distribution environments.
Digit is designed for existing human facilities and has a 35 pound carrying capacity, four hour battery life and continuous-shift capability.
Arc is Agility's cloud automation and fleet-management platform, connecting Digit with AMRs and WMS/MES/WES/PLC systems.

positioningFacts.value: Boston Dynamics positions Atlas as an enterprise-grade industrial humanoid for autonomous material handling, barcode/RFID workflow integration and order fulfillment.
The current product version is fully electric, supports autonomous, VR-teleoperated and tablet operating modes, and integrates with MES/WMS through Orbit.
Boston Dynamics says Atlas deployments are committed for 2026 with Hyundai and Google DeepMind, with additional customers planned for 2027.

positioningFacts.value: Tesla describes Optimus as a general-purpose, bi-pedal, autonomous humanoid robot intended for unsafe, repetitive or boring tasks.
Tesla says the software stack must provide balance, navigation, perception and physical-world interaction.
Tesla's Q2 2024 update reported that Optimus had begun performing tasks autonomously in one Tesla facility, including handling batteries.
